tracks.meaning: a group of actors or performers in a theatrical production, or the actors collectively in a particular role
to throw or fling something with force
to throw a fishing line and hook into water
a rigid mold or support applied to an injured body part to keep it immobilized during healing
a group of muscles or body structures, such as the lateral cast of muscles on one side of the body
an object made by pouring molten metal into a mold and allowing it to solidify
metal type or a set of type characters for printing
a hardened mold or replica of a fossil or natural form created by minerals filling a cavity
the material removed from a snail, worm, or other organism, such as worm castings
a group of people, especially those with particular characteristics or opinions considered as a unit
to set loose or release a boat or ship from moorings
a slight trace or suggestion of a quality or expression, such as a cast of melancholy
a settled disposition or tendency of mind or character
the distance something travels or is thrown, as in 'within a stone's cast'
